top_signals.title: Army launches new Indo-Pacific multi-domain command
top_signals.summary: The move combines traditional formations with some of the service’s cyber, space, unmanned systems and electronic warfare capabilities.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Franklin Templeton files for ETFs that reinvest stock dividends into bitcoin
top_signals.summary: Franklin has filed for two new ETFs that would reinvest stock dividends into BTC, with an expected effective date as early as Sept. 1, 2026.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Solar Is the Cheapest Power in History, But States Are Retreating From It
top_signals.summary: A high-stakes push-and-pull is taking place in the United States clean energy sector as state-level laws, federal-level policy, and that infamous invisible hand all tug in different directions. A staggering amount of different legal actions on the part of the Trump administration, the judicial system, and state politicians have created an incredibly complex landscape for clean energy adoption across the country. And while the economics of renewables are looking better than ever, the political positioning of the clean energy transition is looking…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Would a £10bn VAT cut really save hospitality?
top_signals.summary: Calls for the government to slash VAT on hospitality firms have been backed by pub and restaurant bosses, and Prime Minister hopeful Andy Burnham. What would this tax break really mean for the economy? The UK’s pubs, hotels and restaurants say they are at breaking point. Higher business rates, hikes to national insurance and minimum [...]
